jira_integration_guide.overview:
This guide provides detailed instructions for integrating with Jira using the MCP server to fetch user stories, update issues, and manage the development workflow.
IMPORTANT: The Jira MCP server is deployed in OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) and accessed remotely via SSE (Server-Sent Events). This is NOT a local server.

available_mcp_tools.important:
ALWAYS use these exact tool names when calling the Jira MCP server (mcp-atlassian). Do NOT guess or invent tool names. If a tool call fails, check this list first.
CRITICAL: The MCP server name is "mcp-atlassian" NOT "jira"

tool.name: jira_get_issue
tool.description: Fetch a single Jira issue by key

tool.name: jira_add_comment
tool.description: Add a comment to a Jira issue
tool.note: The correct tool name is jira_add_comment with parameter "body" NOT "comment"

tool.name: jira_transition_issue
tool.description: Move an issue to a new workflow state (e.g. Done)
tool.note: Use jira_get_transitions first to get valid transition IDs

tool.name: jira_get_transitions
tool.description: List available workflow transitions for an issue
tool.usage: Call this before jira_transition_issue to get valid transition IDs

authentication.note: IBM Bob authenticates to the Jira MCP server using the API key configured in local `.bob/mcp.json`.
Upstream Jira credentials are handled server-side by the remote OCP MCP server.

mcp_server_config.note: CRITICAL: Always use "mcp-atlassian" as the server_name, NOT "jira"

step.title: Identify Issue to Work On
step.description: Determine which Jira issue needs to be processed. This can be: - Explicitly provided by user (e.g., "Work on KAN-2") - Selected from a list of available issues - The next issue in the sprint backlog

step.title: Use MCP Tools to Fetch Issue
step.description: Use the Jira MCP server tools to retrieve issue details. The MCP server provides tools for: - Listing issues in a project - Getting detailed issue information - Searching for specific issues - Fetching issue comments and attachments
step.example_usage: When user says "Work on KAN-2" or "Implement the feature in KAN-2", use the MCP Jira tools to fetch the complete issue details including: - Issue summary and description - Acceptance criteria - Explicit automated deployment directive, if present - Exact target OpenShift namespace, if present - Story points and priority - Current status and assignee - Comments and history - Linked issues and dependencies

step.title: Parse Issue Details
step.description: Extract and structure the relevant information from the Jira issue

key_information.item: Summary - Brief description of the feature/task
Acceptance Criteria - Specific conditions that must be met
Deployment Directive - Exact statement saying whether automated deployment is required
Target Namespace - Exact OpenShift namespace when deployment is required

issue_type.name: Story
issue_type.description: User stories describing features from user perspective
issue_type.focus: - Understand user value and business context - Identify acceptance criteria - Plan implementation approach - Consider user experience

issue_type.name: Bug
issue_type.description: Defects or issues in existing functionality
issue_type.focus: - Understand the problem and reproduction steps - Identify root cause - Implement fix - Add regression tests - Verify fix resolves the issue

issue_type.name: Task
issue_type.focus: - Understand technical requirements - Follow best practices - Document changes - Update relevant documentation

issue_type.name: Epic
issue_type.description: Large features broken into multiple stories
issue_type.focus: - Understand overall epic scope - Identify which stories to work on - Ensure consistency across related stories - Track progress toward epic completion

parsing_acceptance_criteria.overview: Acceptance criteria define the conditions that must be satisfied for the story to be considered complete. They are typically written in Given-When-Then format or as a checklist.

format.name: Given-When-Then
format.example: Given I am a logged-in user When I click the "Submit Order" button Then the order should be processed And I should see a confirmation message And I should receive an email confirmation
format.parsing_approach: - Extract preconditions (Given) - Identify actions (When) - List expected outcomes (Then/And) - Map to test scenarios

format.name: Checklist
format.example: - [ ] User can submit an order - [ ] Order is validated before processing - [ ] Confirmation message is displayed - [ ] Email notification is sent - [ ] Order appears in user's order history
format.parsing_approach: - Treat each item as a requirement - Ensure all items are addressed - Verify each item can be tested

format.name: Narrative
format.parsing_approach: - Extract key requirements from text - Identify must-have vs nice-to-have features - Convert to actionable tasks - Clarify ambiguities with user if needed

validation.check: All acceptance criteria are clear and testable
Criteria cover both happy path and edge cases
Dependencies on other stories are noted

when_to_update.scenario: When starting work on an issue
When moving issue through workflow states

update_type.name: add_comment
update_type.best_practices: - Provide clear, concise updates - Include relevant technical details - Mention any blockers or issues - Link to related commits or PRs - Tag relevant team members

example_comments.comment: Implementation complete. Added new payment processing service with support for credit cards and PayPal. All unit tests passing.
Blocked: Need clarification on error handling for failed payments. Should we retry automatically or require user action?
Code review complete. Addressed all feedback. Ready for testing.

common_updates.field: Assignee - Assign to yourself when starting work
Story Points - Update if complexity changes

mcp_update_workflow.step: Use MCP Jira tools to update issue
Confirm changes are visible in Jira UI

practice.title: Always Fetch Latest Issue Data
practice.description: Before starting work, fetch the latest issue details to ensure you have current information including any recent comments or updates.

practice.title: Validate Issue is Ready for Development
practice.description: Check that the issue has: - Clear acceptance criteria - Necessary design documents - No blocking dependencies - Appropriate priority and story points

practice.title: Keep Jira Updated
practice.description: Regularly update the issue status and add comments to keep the team informed of progress, blockers, and completion.

practice.title: Link Related Work
practice.description: When creating commits, PRs, or branches, reference the Jira issue key (e.g., KAN-2) to maintain traceability.

practice.title: Handle Missing Information Gracefully
practice.description: If acceptance criteria or requirements are unclear: - Add a comment in Jira requesting clarification - Ask the user for additional context - Make reasonable assumptions and document them - Proceed with what's clear while waiting for clarification

error.symptoms: Unable to connect to Jira, 401 errors

solutions.solution: Verify API token is valid and not expired
Ensure MCP server configuration is correct
Test connection with simple API call
Verify issue key is correct (e.g., KAN-2 not KAN2)
Check issue exists in the project
Ensure you have permission to view the issue
Try listing all issues to find the correct key
Verify you have edit permissions for the project
Check if issue is in a locked state
Ensure workflow allows the transition you're attempting
Contact project admin if permissions are needed
Verify MCP server URL is accessible
Try again after a brief wait
Check if Jira service is experiencing issues

example_workflow.scenario: User asks to work on KAN-2

step.action: Use MCP Jira tools to fetch issue KAN-2

step.expected_result: Show user: - Issue summary - Description - Acceptance criteria - Current status
Code changes made according to requirements

step.action: Add comment to Jira with implementation summary
Update issue status to "Code Review" or "Done"
step.expected_result: Issue moved to next workflow state
